Bit by bit the charter commits.
sioners let night tried to hammer
something into the new city charter
that will protect the people in the
granting of and the operation under
public service franchises.
Able legal counsel for the corpor.
ations tried to becloud the issues
with talg Of unconstitutionality i'm.
practicability, etc., and fought every
provision that looked like giving
justice to the people. In this work
the corporation representatives
were aided by H. M. Stephens, one
of the two lawyers on the commits
sion. Stephens fought against the
injection of too libertl provisions az
to the control of corporations into
the charter, while John E. Blair, the
other attorney on the commission,
differed with them and insisted that
the strict provisions for the control
of the corporations go in. The labor
representatives fought for adequate
control of franchises and while not
getting what they wanted, secured
some concessions.


As the matter stands, It is said
that corporations are satisfied with
the provisions. They are a little
more restrictive than under the
present charter, but the difference
is not such as to cause the corpora.
tons to 60 out Of business, as was
threatened. As tentatively agreed
upon, the provisions run.


1-Publication in official news
paper.


2-No monopoly.


= 3-Location and extent to be des-
fgnated
